patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 2/2] btrfs: test qgroup deletion races with squota writes

version_history.subject: [PATCH 2/2] btrfs: test qgroup deletion races with squota writes

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch adds four test cases to the Btrfs file system to cover various race conditions between qgroup deletion and squota writes. The tests aim to identify potential bugs in the handling of extent ownership references (EXTENT_OWNER_REF) when a subvolume is deleted or its qgroup is destroyed. The scenarios tested include freeing an extent whose owner qgroup is gone, creating an extent while the owning subvolume is being deleted, destroying a live subvolume's qgroup with delayed refs, and destroying a qgroup belonging to a still-mounted subvolume. The patch ensures that these operations are properly handled by the Btrfs kernel to prevent underflows or transaction aborts.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 1/2] btrfs: inline enable_quota helper in test 301

version_history.subject: [PATCH 1/2] btrfs: inline enable_quota helper in test 301

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch inlines the enable_quota helper function in test 301, removing a dead-weight dispatch and indirection to directly call 'btrfs quota enable --simple'. The change is limited to this specific test and does not affect other parts of the btrfs codebase.

review_comments.author: Zorro Lang
review_comments.summary: Expressed neutrality towards the patch, suggesting separate review of this patch and its companion. Did not raise any specific technical concerns or objections.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 05:43:33PM -0700, Boris Burkov wrote:
> The enable_quota helper in btrfs/301 takes a mode argument ('n', 's',
> or anything else) to switch between "no quota", "simple quota", and
> "full quota" enable.  Only the simple-quota path is ever exercised by
> this test, so the dispatch is dead weight and the indirection just
> obscures the actual btrfs command being run.
> 
> Inline the two call sites to call 'btrfs quota enable --simple'
> directly and drop the helper.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Boris Burkov <boris@bur.io>
> ---

Hahah, to be honest, the title '[PATCH 0/2] squota delete fstests' gave me
a minor heart attack. I thought it was a critical bug report about fstests
accidentally deleting itself while running :-D

>  tests/btrfs/301 | 14 ++------------
>  1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 12 deletions(-)
> 
> diff --git a/tests/btrfs/301 b/tests/btrfs/301
> index 1f72a97b..31243aab 100755
> --- a/tests/btrfs/301
> +++ b/tests/btrfs/301
> @@ -169,16 +169,6 @@ do_enospc_falloc()
>  	do_falloc $file $sz
>  }
>  
> -enable_quota()
> -{
> -	local mode=$1
> -
> -	[ $mode == "n" ] && return
> -	arg=$([ $mode == "s" ] && echo "--simple")
> -
> -	$BTRFS_UTIL_PROG quota enable $arg $SCRATCH_MNT
> -}
> -
>  get_subvid()
>  {
>  	_btrfs_get_subvolid $SCRATCH_MNT subv
> @@ -198,7 +188,7 @@ prepare()
>  {
>  	_scratch_mkfs >> $seqres.full
>  	_scratch_mount
> -	enable_quota "s"
> +	$BTRFS_UTIL_PROG quota enable --simple $SCRATCH_MNT
>  	$BTRFS_UTIL_PROG subvolume create $subv >> $seqres.full
>  	local subvid=$(get_subvid)
>  	set_subvol_limit $subvid $limit
> @@ -421,7 +411,7 @@ enable_mature()
>  	# Sync before enabling squotas to reliably *not* count the writes
>  	# we did before enabling.
>  	sync
> -	enable_quota "s"
> +	$BTRFS_UTIL_PROG quota enable --simple $SCRATCH_MNT

This change doesn't seem related to patch 2/2. I think we can review
these 2 patches separately.

For this patch, I'm fine with or without it. If the btrfs list prefers
to have it, or move it to be a common helper, I'm good with that.

Thanks,
Zorro

>  	set_subvol_limit $subvid $limit
>  	_scratch_cycle_mount
>  	usage=$(get_subvol_usage $subvid)
> -- 
> 2.54.0
> 
>

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 0/2] squota delete fstests
patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series resurrects a previous attempt to land tests for squota qgroup deletion edge cases, incorporating additional testing and feedback.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 4/4] btrfs: swallow btrfs_record_squota_delta() enoent
patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series aims to harden the squota deletion mechanism in Btrfs, making it impossible for an extent to be accounted to a qgroup that outlives its qgroup. The author has re-considered their previous stance on this issue due to recent bugs and believes adding extra protection is necessary. The patch modifies the btrfs_record_squota_delta() function to swallow ENOENT errors, effectively masking real bugs.

review_comments.author: Qu Wenruo
review_comments.summary: Requested adding a WARN_ON_ONCE to catch real bugs in debug builds, rather than masking them by swallowing ENOENT errors.
review_comments.raw_body:
在 2026/5/13 05:04, Boris Burkov 写道:
> I thought that it was likely I could harden squota deletion to the point
> that it was impossible to end up with an extent accounted to a qgroup
> outliving its qgroup. Several recent bugs have made me re-consider that
> position.
> 
> Ultimately, this is a tradeoff between short term stability and long
> term strictness, but I think given that there could be another layer of
> bugs behind the 2-3 I just fixed, I would feel much more confident in
> people using squotas if the risk was "your values can get a bit out of
> whack which you can fix by deleting stuff or
> disabling/re-enabling/repairing" vs "it will abort your filesystem".
> 
> As the final nail in the coffin, the Meta production kernel was lacking
> earlier fixes from me and Qu regarding subvol qgroup lifetime, so this
> is what we have been testing at scale, so I think at least for now
> upstream should have the same extra layer of protection.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Boris Burkov <boris@bur.io>
> ---
>   fs/btrfs/qgroup.c | 2 +-
>   1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)
> 
> diff --git a/fs/btrfs/qgroup.c b/fs/btrfs/qgroup.c
> index a93d49015fc7..f9e5005028dc 100644
> --- a/fs/btrfs/qgroup.c
> +++ b/fs/btrfs/qgroup.c
> @@ -4959,7 +4959,7 @@ int btrfs_record_squota_delta(struct btrfs_fs_info *fs_info,
>   	spin_lock(&fs_info->qgroup_lock);
>   	qgroup = find_qgroup_rb(fs_info, root);
>   	if (!qgroup) {
> -		ret = -ENOENT;
> +		ret = 0;

I appreciate that end users won't be bothered with transaction abort, 
but on the other hand, this seems to mask real bugs.

Can we add a WARN_ON_ONCE(IS_ENABLED(CONFIG_BTRFS_DEBUG))) so that we 
can catch such problems for debug builds?

Thanks,
Qu>   		goto out;
>   	}
>

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 3/4] btrfs: clamp to avoid squota underflow

version_history.subject: [PATCH 3/4] btrfs: clamp to avoid squota underflow

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series addresses quota accounting issues in Btrfs, specifically a problem where metadata tree block allocations can be undercounted in certain scenarios, leading to qgroups becoming permanently orphaned after subvolume deletion. The patch introduces clamping of rfer and excl values to zero on underflow to mitigate this issue.

review_comments.author: Qu Wenruo
review_comments.summary: Suggested adding a WARN_ON_ONCE() to increase the likelihood of detecting undercounted metadata tree block allocations in fstests.
review_comments.raw_body:
在 2026/5/13 05:04, Boris Burkov 写道:
> Simple quota accounting can undercount metadata tree block allocations
> in certain scenarios. When an undercounted subvolume is deleted and its
> tree blocks freed, the free deltas decrement rfer/excl past zero,
> wrapping the u64 to a value near U64_MAX.
> 
> Once wrapped, can_delete_squota_qgroup() sees non-zero rfer and refuses
> to delete the qgroup. The qgroup becomes permanently orphaned in the
> quota tree, since there is no subvolume left to generate frees that
> would bring the counter back to zero.
> 
> While we ultimately want to fix any mis-accounting at the source, it is
> also helpful and worthwhile to mitigate the damage by clamping rfer and
> excl to zero on underflow rather than allowing the u64 to wrap. This at
> least allows us to clean up the messed up qgroups on subvol deletion.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Boris Burkov <boris@bur.io>
> ---
>   fs/btrfs/qgroup.c | 15 +++++++++++++--
>   1 file changed, 13 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
> 
> diff --git a/fs/btrfs/qgroup.c b/fs/btrfs/qgroup.c
> index 96746ca19e17..a93d49015fc7 100644
> --- a/fs/btrfs/qgroup.c
> +++ b/fs/btrfs/qgroup.c
> @@ -4968,8 +4968,19 @@ int btrfs_record_squota_delta(struct btrfs_fs_info *fs_info,
>   	list_for_each_entry(qg, &qgroup_list, iterator) {
>   		struct btrfs_qgroup_list *glist;
>   
> -		qg->excl += num_bytes * sign;
> -		qg->rfer += num_bytes * sign;
> +		ASSERT(qg->excl == qg->rfer);
> +		if (sign < 0 && qg->excl < num_bytes) {
> +			btrfs_warn(fs_info,
> +				   "squota underflow qg %hu/%llu excl %llu num_bytes %llu",
> +				   btrfs_qgroup_level(qg->qgroupid),
> +				   btrfs_qgroup_subvolid(qg->qgroupid),
> +				   qg->excl, num_bytes);

This may not be noisy enough for fstests to catch.

Maybe a WARN_ON_ONCE()?

Thanks,
Qu

> +			qg->excl = 0;
> +			qg->rfer = 0;
> +		} else {
> +			qg->excl += num_bytes * sign;
> +			qg->rfer += num_bytes * sign;
> +		}
>   		qgroup_dirty(fs_info, qg);
>   
>   		list_for_each_entry(glist, &qg->groups, next_group)

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 2/4] btrfs: fix squota accounting during enable generation

version_history.subject: [PATCH 2/4] btrfs: fix squota accounting during enable generation

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch fixes a problem in Btrfs where quota accounting is incorrect when enabling quotas for the first time. When the first transaction that enables quotas completes, it sets a flag to indicate that quotas are enabled, but this can cause a deadlock and lead to incorrect quota values. The patch introduces two changes: first, it sets the enable generation to the next transaction's ID instead of the current one, ensuring that extents written during the enabling transaction are not accounted for; second, it adds a new flag to record squota deltas for extents written between the end of the enabling transaction and when the quotas-enabled flag is set. This fixes an underflow issue where metadata tree blocks would be freed prematurely, causing mismatched usage errors in btrfs check.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 1/4] btrfs: check for subvolume before deleting squota qgroup

version_history.subject: [PATCH 1/4] btrfs: check for subvolume before deleting squota qgroup

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the Btrfs file system where deleting a subvolume quota (squota) group could break an invariant, leading to inconsistent state. The fix unifies the deletion check logic for both full qgroups and squotas, ensuring that the correct checks are performed before deleting a subvolume quota group. This prevents potential issues with shared extents outliving their owning subvolumes. The patch modifies the `can_delete_parent_qgroup` function to perform additional checks specific to squota groups, including verifying that there is no root and checking the usage of the parent qgroup.

patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH 0/4] btrfs: squota lifetime fixes
patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch series aims to strengthen the enforcement of a crucial invariant in btrfs squotas: 'a squota qgroup must outlast extents that are accounted to it'. It consists of two fixes for squota lifetime bugs and two patches that reduce the consequences of errors in lifetime management. The series also includes additional improvements to btrfs check and fstests.

review_comments.author: Qu Wenruo
review_comments.summary: Identified potential issues in the last two patches and suggested adding a WARN_ON_ONCE() to catch real bugs for debug builds. Considered the patch series overall and found it looks good, but minor comments were made.

> Simple quota accounting can undercount metadata tree block allocations
> in certain scenarios. When an undercounted subvolume is deleted and its

> @@ -4968,8 +4968,19 @@ int btrfs_record_squota_delta(struct btrfs_fs_info *fs_info,
>   	list_for_each_entry(qg, &qgroup_list, iterator) {
>   		struct btrfs_qgroup_list *glist;

> -		qg->rfer += num_bytes * sign;

> +		if (sign < 0 && qg->excl < num_bytes) {

> +				   "squota underflow qg %hu/%llu excl %llu num_bytes %llu",

> +				   qg->excl, num_bytes);

This may not be noisy enough for fstests to catch.

Thanks,
Qu

> +			qg->rfer = 0;
> +		} else {
> +			qg->excl += num_bytes * sign;
> +			qg->rfer += num_bytes * sign;
> +		}
>   		qgroup_dirty(fs_info, qg);

review_comments.author: David Sterba
review_comments.summary: Expressed general agreement with the patch series and suggested adding it to for-next.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Tue, May 12, 2026 at 12:34:52PM -0700, Boris Burkov wrote:
> A recent report of read-only filesystems since enabling squotas:
> 
> https://lore.kernel.org/linux-btrfs/adnBhWfJQ1n3hZC8@merlins.org/
> 
> led me to investigate a handful of issues in squotas and find some trivial and
> not so trivial bugs in their lifetime management. The aim of the series is to
> strengthen enforcement of the invariant: "a squota qgroup must outlast extents
> that are accounted to it".
> 
> This series consists of two fixes to squota lifetime bugs and two patches that
> reduce the consequences of errors in lifetime management from full fs goes
> read-only abort to "warning and inaccurate squotas which can be deleted
> eventually"
> 
> Additional improvements to btrfs check and fstests to follow.
> 
> Boris Burkov (4):
>   btrfs: check for subvolume before deleting squota qgroup
>   btrfs: fix squota accounting during enable generation
>   btrfs: clamp to avoid squota underflow
>   btrfs: swallow btrfs_record_squota_delta() enoent

Looks good to me, as much I can understand it. Please add it to
for-next.
